name: Water Cave from Huelga Utrera - Cazorla, Segura and Las Villas S. N. P.
name_original: Cueva del Agua desde Huelga Utrera - P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas
region_intro: Hiking around Segura De La Sierra offers access to the vast Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. The region is characterized by diverse terrain, including valleys, limestone plateaus, deep gorges, and towering mountains. Extensive pine forests cover much of the landscape, which is also notable as the source of the Guadalquivir and Segura rivers. Karstic formations of dolomitic limestone are prominent features throughout the area. Best hiking trails around Segura De La Sierra The most popular hiking route is Moralejos de Arriba from Seguro de la Sierra - circular through the P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas, a 9.8 miles (15.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 47 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the natural park's varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local hikers is El Yelmo - peak in the Cazorla, Segura and Las Villas Sierras National Park, a difficult 11.7 miles (18.9 km) path. This trail leads to a prominent peak within the natural park, offering expansive views. Local hikers also love the Water Cave from Huelga Utrera - Cazorla, Segura and Las Villas S. N. P., a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail leading through diverse terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 31 minutes. Hiking around Segura De La Sierra is defined by dense pine forests, dramatic karstic formations, and river sources. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate walks to challenging climbs. The routes in Segura De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 27 reviews. More than 180 hikers have used komoot to explore Segura De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Segura De La Sierra?
A: Segura De La Sierra, located within the vast Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park, offers incredibly diverse landscapes. You'll encounter verdant pine forests, including unique species like the laricio pine, dramatic valleys, limestone plateaus known as "calares," and deep gorges. The region is also characterized by magnificent karstic formations of dolomitic limestone, and is the source of two of Spain's longest rivers, the Guadalquivir and the Segura.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for hiking trails in Segura De La Sierra?
A: The hiking trails in Segura De La Sierra cater to a range of abilities. You'll find a good selection of moderate routes, perfect for a solid day out, as well as several challenging paths for experienced hikers seeking more strenuous climbs. Currently, there are no easy routes listed, so be prepared for some elevation gain.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Segura De La Sierra?
A: Yes, Segura De La Sierra offers several excellent circular routes. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984773935/moralejos-de-arriba-from-seguro-de-la-sierra-circular-through-the-p-n-de-las-sierras-de-cazorla-segura-y-las-villas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moralejos de Arriba from Seguro de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 9.8-mile trail exploring the natural park's varied landscapes. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14623082" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arab Baths – Segura de la Sierra loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 8.7-mile route.

Q: Can I find trails that lead to mountain peaks or offer panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. The region is home to several prominent peaks offering expansive views. A challenging route leads to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983370013/el-yelmo-peak-in-the-cazorla-segura-and-las-villas-sierras-national-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Yelmo&lt;/a&gt;, a significant summit within the natural park. Other notable peaks in the area include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5182824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mentiras&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5182879" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Palomera&lt;/a&gt;, which can be explored via the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983005177/peak-lies-and-palomera-crag-p-n-of-the-sierra-de-cazorla-segura-y-las-villas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peak Lies and Palomera Crag&lt;/a&gt; trail.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or caves to explore along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the karstic geology of Segura De La Sierra creates impressive natural features. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5335467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Agua&lt;/a&gt; (Water Cave), which is featured on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e982961478/water-cave-from-huelga-utrera-cazorla-segura-and-las-villas-s-n-p" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Water Cave from Huelga Utrera&lt;/a&gt; trail. Other notable waterfalls in the area include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5299889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saltaor Waterfall (Zurriero)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3937194" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Humo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Segura De La Sierra?
A: The Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park offers beautiful hiking opportunities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for hiking, with vibrant flora in spring and stunning colors in autumn. Summers can be hot, especially at lower elevations, while winters can bring colder temperatures and occasional snow at higher altitudes.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Segura De La Sierra?
A: The trails in Segura De La Sierra are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 out of 5 stars from over 27 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's dense pine forests, dramatic karstic formations, and the well-maintained network of paths that offer diverse experiences for various skill levels.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking routes in the region?
A: Yes, Segura De La Sierra is traversed by several significant long-distance trails. These include the GR 144 (Transhumance in the Sierra de Segura), GR 146 (Mountains and olive groves), GR 147 (La Sierra de Segura Profunda), and the GR 247 "Bosques del Sur" (Southern Forests). The GR 247 is particularly notable for its extensive journey through well-preserved pine forests, offering a truly immersive experience.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while hiking?
A: As part of a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, the region boasts rich fauna. Hikers have opportunities to spot Spanish ibex, deer, wild boar, and mouflon in their natural habitats. Birdwatching is also popular, given the diverse ecosystems.

Q: Is Segura De La Sierra suitable for family-friendly hikes?
A: While the guide primarily lists moderate and difficult routes, the broader Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park does offer options for families. Look for shorter, less strenuous sections of trails or specific local paths around villages that might be more suitable for children. Always check the route details for distance and elevation before setting out with family.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Segura De La Sierra?
A: Generally, dogs are permitted on trails within the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park, but they should be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Always be mindful of local regulations, especially in protected areas, and ensure you clean up after your pet.

Q: What is the geological significance of the area?
A: The geology of Segura De La Sierra is dominated by magnificent karstic formations of dolomitic limestone. These unique structures have been shaped by the dissolving action of water over millennia, creating features like the impressive Cerrada de Elías gorge, the Salto de los Órganos waterfall, and various caves such as the Los Anguijones Caves.
tips: La Huelga Utrera is a beautiful, well-preserved village of about 30 inhabitants, located at the junctions of the Segura and Madera rivers and crossed by streams in its streets. Celebrate festivals in honor of the Virgin of Fatima in the third week of August.
Spectacular views of much of the environment of the Segura river, on its way from Poyotello to the Huelga Utrera.

Beautiful waterfall that when hitting the rocks forms a thick mist. This fact makes the place known as the Charco del Humo. One more singularity of this beautiful corner of Segura Pontones where all this land is a hymn to the wonders of nature.

At the entrance to Poyotello –a small and singular village belonging to the municipality of Santiago Pontones– a path starts that continuously descends and will take us to the water cave.
From the Cueva del Agua we can continue the path, which always runs parallel to the Segura riverbed until it ends at the village of Huelga Utrera.
